ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific 'iced chocolate drink' entry; closest match is chocolate beverage (FOODON_03301710). Iced/chilled preparation variant not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers chocolate-flavoured drinks broadly; no specific iced chocolate sub-code exists. Used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for iced chocolate beverage; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Premium Iced Chocolate is approximately 250 g (one cup or glass), as typically served in a café or food-service setting in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Iced Chocolate Drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for iced chocolate drink (cr_id G00010974). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Protein is reported as 0.0 g/100g by HPB; this reflects the diluted beverage formulation where milk protein contribution is negligible at the measured concentration. Fat is 0.25 g/100g as reported.

gi_source.title: Chocolate milk and chocolate-flavoured beverages — GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to iced chocolate drink
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Premium Iced Chocolate' in the University of Sydney GI Database. The closest analogous entry is chocolate milk / chocolate-flavoured milk drink, which has a reported GI of approximately 34–64 depending on sugar content and preparation. Given the high sugar content of this product (9.94 g/100g) and the diluted, predominantly sugar-and-water matrix (low protein, low fat), a GI of 60 (medium) is applied as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 25.9) / 100 ≈ 15.5, based on 250 g serving containing 25.9 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (9.94 g/100g); sugar-sweetened beverages independently raise blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened iced chocolate beverage; the AHA (2018) identifies added sugar in beverages as an independent blood pressure risk factor, and the low sodium content (3 mg/100g) does not offset the adverse effect of 9.94 g sugar per 100 g; an unsweetened cocoa preparation with low-fat milk and no added sugar is the recommended alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100g and 0 g dietary fibre; liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; at 9.94 g sugar per 100 g with zero dietary fibre and a medium GI of approximately 60, a 250 g serving delivers approximately 24.9 g of rapidly absorbed sugar, causing a significant postprandial glucose spike; an unsweetened iced cocoa made with low-fat milk and no added sugar is the clinically appropriate alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar load from a sugar-sweetened beverage; regular consumption linked to elevated triglycerides and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened iced chocolate drink; habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased LDL particle density, and higher CVD event risk (AHA Dietary Guidelines); the saturated fat content is low (estimated 0.16 g/100g) and sodium is minimal, but the sugar burden (9.94 g/100g) is the primary cardiovascular concern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar (9.94 g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators and negates the potential anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of cocoa
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened iced chocolate; the high added sugar content promotes inflammatory cytokine production and offsets any anti-inflammatory benefit from cocoa polyphenols; an unsweetened dark cocoa preparation with no added sugar would be the RA-appropriate alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Premium Iced Chocolate is a chilled, sugar-sweetened chocolate beverage served in cafés and food-service outlets in Singapore and across the Western-influenced café culture of Southeast Asia. It is prepared by dissolving cocoa powder and sugar in milk or water, then serving over ice to produce a cold, refreshing drink. The beverage has a rich chocolate flavour with a sweet, slightly creamy taste and a thin, liquid texture. Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and negligible protein (0.0 g as reported by HPB, reflecting the diluted beverage matrix). Dietary fibre is absent (0.0 g/100g), sodium is very low at 3 mg/100g, and potassium is minimal at 5 mg/100g. The high sugar-to-fibre ratio and sugar-sweetened beverage classification make this drink clinically significant: it is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to rapid postprandial glucose elevation, independent blood pressure risk from added sugar, and elevated triglyceride risk from habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium) based on analogous chocolate milk entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 15.5 per 250 g serving. Cocoa provides trace iron (0.02 mg/100g) and minimal calcium (1 mg/100g). An unsweetened version made with low-fat milk and no added sugar would substantially improve the nutritional and clinical profile of this beverage. Known in Singapore by multiple names: 优质冰巧克力 (Chinese), Coklat Ais Premium (Malay), பிரீமியம் ஐஸ் சாக்லேட் (Tamil), and प्रीमियम आइस्ड चॉकलेट (Hindi).
common_user_queries: How many calories in iced chocolate?
Is iced chocolate high in sugar?
What is the GI of chocolate milk drink?
Is iced chocolate suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010974, confidence 0.95). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are preserved from the HPB record. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (chocolate milk category) and is clearly documented as such.
notes: Zero-macro justification (g4): protein_g is reported as 0.0 g/100g by HPB for this iced chocolate beverage; this reflects the highly diluted beverage formulation where the milk protein contribution falls below the reportable threshold in the HPB database — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo (G00010974). Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g/100g — confirmed zero per HPB; this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing ingredients at measurable levels. Magnesium_mg is 0.0 mg/100g — confirmed zero per HPB. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 mg/100g — confirmed zero per HPB; the fat content is very low (0.25 g/100g) and cholesterol is below detection. Trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as no trans fat sources are present in this beverage formulation. Saturated_fat_g is estimated at 0.16 g/100g based on the fat profile of a diluted milk-and-cocoa beverage (approximately 65% of total fat as saturated, consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for chocolate milk beverages); this is an approximation as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. Negative sentinel values (g3): monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for this specific iced chocolate beverage are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database entry G00010974, and no equivalent approved secondary source provides these values for this exact product. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are set to -1: the HPB Foodo database entry G00010974 does not report a full micronutrient vitamin panel for this beverage; no approved secondary source (USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD) provides these values for this exact iced chocolate formulation. phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: mineral panel data beyond potassium, calcium, magnesium, and iron are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry G00010974 for this beverage, and no approved secondary source provides these values for this exact product. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database entry G00010974 for this beverage. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples / Baking ingredients' to 'Beverages / Chocolate and cocoa beverages' as this is a ready-to-drink chilled beverage, not a baking ingredient. Multilingual local names added for G6 compliance: this beverage is served in Singapore and the record region_tag is SG; accordingly, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names have been provided as translations of the canonical English name.
